textPlain: This is, as they say, "The beginning of the end."

While GitHub had previously started work on migrating parts of its service to Azure, our understanding is that these migrations have been halting and sometimes failedAnd there's no reason to suspect this next batch of migrations will be any different. Telling your engineers, 'good luck, you get to spend the next 18 months treading water,' is a terrible way to get them to give their best or even stick around.
nah, I'd say we're well past that. The beginning might have been Microsoft's acquisition of GitHub. Or the elimination of GitHub's independence.

Beginning of the end of what? If I could have take a bet, “Will GitHub move to Azure?” a few years ago, I would have thrown money down.This seems inevitable since the acquisition and not necessarily a bad thing. I see it as neutral.
